purpose: Authoritative source of truth for every state, transition, action, artifact, and event in FlowBench. Validates workflows.json, actions.json, state-machine tests, API responses, and UI rendering.

ownership_boundary.description: FlowBench is the system of record for workflow state, state transitions, artifacts, events, and run lifecycle. Adapters do not own workflow truth. They return execution results that FlowBench validates, persists, and translates into state changes according to the workflow contract.

notes: Approval model: stage-level only. Risk categories are declared per-action in actions.json, not detected from adapter output. Approval is a pre-dispatch gate — the builder confirms the category of risk before the adapter runs.
Recovery: every terminal or blocked state has at least one recovery action. No dead ends.
Run persistence: every adapter-backed action creates a RunRecord. Interrupted runs are detected on startup and surfaced to the builder.
Phase 2 = console shell only. Phase 4 = all artifact renderers and timeline. No overlap.

Backend is the safety authority for approval. The UI is a convenience layer. A risky action is never accepted merely because a client claims it was previously displayed in a confirmation dialog. The backend independently checks the confirmed flag against the configured risk_category for the requested action.

Phase 1 does not implement adapter-backed actions. POST /actions for adapter-backed actions returns adapter_not_available until Phase 3. Only state-only actions (action_type: system or navigation) work in Phase 1.

artifact_layout.description: V1 layout: all FlowBench state lives in a single .flowbench/ directory inside the selected project repo. No project-name subdirectory. Flat artifact namespace (phase-specific artifacts use phase-<id> suffixes). This layout is locked for V1 — a future multi-project feature requires a separate scope decision and must not leak its structure into V1.

contents.current-state.json: Current project and phase state, selected repo path, project display name, adapter name, timestamps
contents.events.ndjson: Append-only event log, one compact JSON object per line
contents.phase-plan-<phase-id>.json: Detailed plan for a specific phase
contents.audit.json: Existing-app audit (present only in existing_app mode)
contents.decision-<id>.json: Decision artifacts for skips, overrides, cancellations

rules: All FlowBench writes stay within .flowbench/ in the selected repository. No writes outside this directory tree.
current-state.json carries the selected repository's normalized absolute path and a project display name.
No project-name subdirectory under .flowbench/ in V1.
Phase-specific artifacts use the pattern <type>-<phase-id>.json (e.g., phase-plan-phase_003.json).
The selected repository is the project boundary. Switching repos is equivalent to starting a new project.
A future multi-project feature must be a separate scope decision and must define its own layout. Do not reserve structure for it now.

safety_constraints: Validate and normalize repo paths before use; resolve symlinks
All artifact writes restricted to .flowbench/ directory inside the selected project repo
Never persist secrets in artifacts, prompts, logs, or UI messages
Treat all artifact content as untrusted text when rendering (escape HTML)
Record command template version and working directory in each RunRecord
Atomic writes: write temp file → fsync → rename into place
Events appended only after durable artifact write succeeds
phase_management_rules: Only upcoming phases can be reordered
A phase cannot move ahead of an unmet dependency (phases are sequential in V1 — reorder is a V1 UI affordance but the queue is always processed in order)
Skipping a phase requires a plain-English reason recorded in a decision artifact
Skipped phases remain visible in the queue with status: skipped
Skipped phases can be restored to upcoming status before project_complete
Any reorder or skip action creates an event log entry and a decision artifact
Override-and-continue in review/test requires a plain-English reason recorded in a decision artifact

sharpness_criteria.description: A master plan or phase plan is considered sharp (ready for acceptance) only when ALL of these are true:
criteria: Acceptance checks are concrete and testable
Builder decisions required during this phase are listed separately
Existing behavior to preserve is stated (in existing-app mode)
The next action after this plan is obvious
Unresolved blockers are either cleared or explicitly called out with their impact

start_new_project.label: Start new project
start_new_project.description: Create a new project from scratch

load_existing_project.label: Load existing app
load_existing_project.description: Point to an existing codebase and audit it
load_existing_project.recovery: audit failed → retry with different path or switch to new build

generate_master_plan.label: Generate master plan
generate_master_plan.description: Create a full project plan from your scope
generate_master_plan.recovery: generation failed → retry or edit scope

cancel_project.label: Cancel project
cancel_project.risk_explanation: This will mark the project as cancelled. All artifacts and history are preserved but the project will be considered complete.

_auto_transition.description: On adapter completion, transitions automatically to master_plan_sharpening or project_blocked

refine_plan.label: Refine the plan
refine_plan.description: Re-generate the master plan with updated context

sharpen_plan.label: Refine the plan
sharpen_plan.description: Ask the execution tool to review the plan and surface ambiguities or missing context

accept_master_plan.label: Accept the plan
accept_master_plan.description: Mark the master plan as sharp and proceed to phase execution

cancel_project.label: Cancel project
cancel_project.risk_explanation: This will mark the project as cancelled. All artifacts and history are preserved.

start_next_phase.label: Start next phase
start_next_phase.description: Begin working on the first upcoming phase

reorder_phases.label: Re-order phases
reorder_phases.description: Change the order of upcoming phases
preconditions: there are at least 2 upcoming phases

phase_in_progress.label: Phase in progress
phase_in_progress.note: This is a passthrough state. The active phase's state machine governs actual behavior. The project-level display shows 'A phase is in progress' and the current phase name.

accept_handoff.label: Accept handoff
accept_handoff.description: Mark this phase as complete and return to the phase queue

replan_from_here.label: Re-plan from here
replan_from_here.description: Go back to the master plan and revise it

revise_scope.label: Revise scope
revise_scope.description: Go back to the scope and change what you want to build

cancel_project.label: Cancel project
cancel_project.risk_explanation: This will mark the project as cancelled. All artifacts and history are preserved.

archive_project.label: Archive project
archive_project.description: Save the project state and start fresh

start_new_project.label: Start a new project
start_new_project.description: Begin a new project (archives current one)
start_new_project.risk_explanation: Starting a new project will archive the current project. Current project artifacts are preserved but it will no longer be active.

generate_phase_plan.label: Generate phase plan
generate_phase_plan.description: Create a detailed plan for this phase only

skip_phase.label: Skip this phase
skip_phase.description: Skip this phase and move to the next one
skip_phase.recovery: skipped phases can be restored to upcoming before project_complete

cancel_phase.label: Cancel phase
cancel_phase.description: Cancel this phase and return to the project queue

_auto_transition.description: On adapter completion, transitions automatically to phase_sharpening or phase_blocked

sharpen_phase_plan.label: Sharpen this plan
sharpen_phase_plan.description: Ask the execution tool to review the phase plan for ambiguities

accept_phase_plan.label: Accept phase plan
accept_phase_plan.description: Mark this phase plan as sharp and prepare to build

start_building.label: Start building
start_building.description: Execute the phase plan — the execution tool will make changes
start_building.risk_explanation: Starting this build may create, modify, or delete files in your project directory. The execution tool will follow the approved phase plan but may make independent decisions about implementation details. Review changes after the build completes.

change_phase_plan.label: Change phase plan
change_phase_plan.description: Go back and revise the phase plan

cancel_phase.label: Cancel phase
cancel_phase.description: Cancel this phase and return to the project queue

_auto_transition.description: On adapter completion, transitions to phase_reviewing (success), or phase_blocked (failure)

pause.label: Pause
pause.description: Stop the current build and inspect progress
pause.recovery: from blocked: retry (restarts build) or re-plan

ask_for_summary.label: Ask for a summary
ask_for_summary.description: Get a progress update from the execution tool

cancel_phase.label: Cancel phase
cancel_phase.description: Cancel this phase and return to the project queue
cancel_phase.risk_explanation: Cancelling during a build may leave partial changes in your project directory. The phase can be restarted later.

_auto_transition.description: Review runs automatically on build completion. Findings are saved as review-findings.json.

accept_review.label: Accept review
accept_review.description: Mark the review as complete and proceed to testing

fix_findings.label: Fix findings
fix_findings.description: Have the execution tool address review findings
fix_findings.risk_explanation: The execution tool will modify files to address review findings.

override_and_continue.label: Override and continue
override_and_continue.description: Ignore review findings and proceed to testing

_auto_transition.description: Tests run automatically. Results saved as test-results.json.

accept_test_results.label: Accept test results
accept_test_results.description: Mark tests as passing and proceed to handoff

fix_failures.label: Fix failures
fix_failures.description: Have the execution tool address test failures
fix_failures.risk_explanation: The execution tool will modify files to fix test failures.

skip_tests.label: Skip tests
skip_tests.description: Skip testing and proceed to handoff

_auto_transition.description: On adapter completion, transitions to phase_reviewing (fix_cycle) or phase_blocked (failure)

pause.label: Pause
pause.description: Stop fixing and inspect the current state

cancel_phase.label: Cancel phase
cancel_phase.description: Cancel this phase and return to the project queue
cancel_phase.risk_explanation: Cancelling during fixing may leave partial changes in your project directory.

generate_handoff.label: Generate handoff notes
generate_handoff.description: Create structured notes for the next phase

accept_handoff.label: Accept handoff
accept_handoff.description: Mark the phase handoff as complete

phase_complete.label: Phase complete

start_next_phase.label: Start next phase
start_next_phase.description: Begin the next phase in the queue

view_handoff_notes.label: View handoff notes
view_handoff_notes.description: Read the handoff artifact for this phase

phase_complete.note: Terminal phase state. Returns control to the project state machine (phase_queue_ready → next phase or project_complete).

replan_phase.label: Re-plan phase
replan_phase.description: Go back to the phase plan and revise it

abandon_phase.label: Abandon phase
abandon_phase.description: Cancel this phase and return to the project queue
abandon_phase.risk_explanation: Abandoning a phase discards any uncompleted work. The phase can be restarted later.

context_bundle_rules.description: For each adapter-backed action, the context bundle builder assembles the required inputs into the command prompt. Listed in order of assembly.

rules.assembly_note: Read-only scan. No context injection needed beyond the repo path.
Scope is the primary input. If in existing_app mode and audit exists, prepend 'Current Project State' section before scope.
Current plan + scope are always required. Sharpening history (previous rounds) included if available. Audit included in existing_app mode.
Master plan + the specific phase definition. Prior phase handoff included if available. Audit included in existing_app mode.
Approved phase plan + any prior handoff for context stability. Master plan excerpt included if relevant.
Compare what was planned against what was built. No project-level context needed — review is scoped to the phase.
Tests should cover the phase plan scope. Review findings indicate what to test specifically.
Findings (from review) or failures (from tests) plus the plan and current build state so fixes stay in scope.
Full phase artifact set. Unresolved issues and next phase context help write a useful handoff.
No injected context. The adapter captures whatever the current execution state is.

adapter_capabilities.description: Descriptive-only mapping of adapter-backed actions. References the exact snake_case action identifiers from the contract. Capability metadata is descriptive only — it cannot modify dispatch, recovery, context assembly, state transitions, or adapter selection.
rules: Capability metadata must reference the existing contract action names exactly (snake_case). No aliases, camelCase variants, or alternate names.
Capability metadata must not introduce fallback behavior, rerouting, or synthesized steps.
Capability metadata must not imply new state machine concepts, artifact types, or workflow actions.

audit_existing_app.summary: Scan an existing codebase to produce a 5-section audit report.

generate_master_plan.summary: Create a full project plan from the scope document.

sharpen_plan.summary: Review the master plan for ambiguities and missing context.

generate_phase_plan.summary: Create a detailed plan for a single phase.

build_phase.summary: Execute a phase plan — the tool produces file changes in the project directory.

review_phase.summary: Compare what was built against the phase plan and produce findings.
review_phase.note: This is its own adapter-backed action, not part of 'planning'.

test_phase.summary: Run tests on the build output and report results.

fix_findings.summary: Address review findings or test failures by modifying files.

generate_handoff.summary: Create structured handoff notes for the next phase.

summarize_state.summary: Request a progress update from the execution tool.
summarize_state.note: Read-only and non-recovery. Does not resume sessions, retry work, or substitute for interrupted-run handling. Recovery is governed separately by RunRecords and the recovery choices defined in this contract.

run_record_schema.description: Every adapter-backed action creates a RunRecord persisted to <repo>/.flowbench/runs/<run-id>.json.

fields.status: string — one of: queued, running, succeeded, failed, timed_out, cancelled, interrupted
fields.input_artifact_refs: object — map of context_key to artifact_path or hash
fields.output_artifact_path: string | null — path to the artifact produced, if any
fields.failure_message: string | null — plain English error description
fields.recovery_message: string | null — plain English suggested next step
fields.template_version: string — hash or version of the command template used
fields.working_directory: string — repo path where the command ran
fields.command_context_hash: string — sha256 of the assembled context bundle inputs (scope, plan, audit, handoff, etc.). Enables tracing a retry back to the prior attempt while confirming it uses a fresh context.

status_transitions: running → succeeded | failed | timed_out | cancelled | interrupted (terminal or interrupt)
terminal states (succeeded, failed, timed_out, cancelled, interrupted) never transition directly back to running
retry creates a NEW RunRecord — it does not change a terminal record's status

recovery_choices.label: Inspect current state
recovery_choices.exact_meaning: Open the last known artifacts, event history, run details, and working directory. Read-only — never changes any state.
recovery_choices.safety_rule: Read-only. Never changes state or transitions. Simply returns current artifact content.

recovery_choices.label: Retry the last action
recovery_choices.exact_meaning: Create a NEW RunRecord and run the same action with a newly assembled current context bundle (fresh scope, current plan, latest handoff — not the stale inputs from the interrupted run). The retry is a new execution, not a continuation.
recovery_choices.safety_rule: Requires approval confirmation again if the action has a risk_category. Must never reuse a stale process, stale output file, or stale approval from the interrupted run. command_context_hash must differ from the interrupted run if the context changed.

recovery_choices.label: Continue from current state
recovery_choices.exact_meaning: Mark the interrupted run as acknowledged (status remains interrupted). Do not execute anything. Present only stage-valid next actions — the builder chooses what to do next based on the current artifacts.
recovery_choices.safety_rule: Cannot force a transition, skip required artifacts, or auto-advance the state. The builder selects the next action from the command pane as normal.

recovery_choices.label: Revise the plan
recovery_choices.exact_meaning: Return to the applicable planning or sharpening action without modifying completed artifacts. Prior artifacts remain immutable and visible.
recovery_choices.safety_rule: Re-planning does not destroy prior artifacts. The builder can always reference what was previously generated.

run_record_schema.recovery_rule: On startup, any RunRecord with status=running is set to status=interrupted. The builder is shown a recovery prompt with the four choices above. Never auto-rerun interrupted work. Only one run can be active at a time — enforce with a single-active-run lock.

golden_path_tests.name: new_build_golden_path
golden_path_tests.description: Full new build lifecycle from init to completion, including recovery after restart
steps: Initialize project → state is starting
Set scope text → state is scope_ready
Generate master plan (mocked adapter) → state is master_plan_sharpening
Sharpen master plan once → state is master_plan_sharpening (iterative)
Accept master plan → state is phase_queue_ready, phase-queue.json has phases
Start first phase → state is phase_in_progress, phase state is phase_starting
Generate phase plan (mocked adapter) → phase state is phase_sharpening
Accept phase plan → phase state is phase_ready_to_build
[SIMULATE RESTART] — verify current-state.json reloads same state
POST /actions/start_building with confirmed:true (mocked adapter) → phase state is phase_reviewing
Accept review → phase state is phase_testing
Accept test results → phase state is phase_handoff
Generate handoff (mocked adapter) → handoff.json created
Accept handoff → phase state is phase_complete, project state is phase_queue_ready
Complete all phases → project state is project_complete

golden_path_tests.name: existing_app_golden_path
golden_path_tests.description: Existing app mode: audit, plan, one complete phase
steps: Create temp directory with known files (e.g., package.json, src/index.ts, tests/)
Initialize project in existing_app mode pointing to temp dir
Verify audit artifact is created with 5 sections
Set scope for a new feature
Generate master plan — verify audit context appears in prompt (mocked adapter)
Sharpen and accept master plan
Complete one full phase: plan → build → review → test → handoff (mocked adapter)
Verify handoff artifact references the phase context correctly
Clean up temp directory

golden_path_tests.name: interrupted_run_recovery
golden_path_tests.description: Verify interrupted run detection and recovery prompt
steps: Start a build action (mocked adapter that hangs)
Stop the FlowBench process mid-run
Restart FlowBench
Verify RunRecord with status=running is now status=interrupted
Verify UI shows recovery prompt (not auto-rerun)

golden_path_tests.name: approval_gate
golden_path_tests.description: Verify stage-level approval blocks risky actions until confirmed
steps: Reach phase_ready_to_build state
POST /actions/start_building without confirmed flag (risk_category: modify_files)
Verify response includes confirmation_required with risk_explanation
Verify state did not change — no adapter call, no RunRecord created
POST /actions/start_building with confirmed: true
Verify adapter is called and state transitions to phase_building

golden_path_tests.name: invalid_transition_rejected
golden_path_tests.description: Verify invalid state transitions return clear errors
steps: In scope_ready, attempt to POST /actions/accept_master_plan
Verify 400 response with plain English explanation
Verify state did not change

golden_path_tests.name: adapter_action_unavailable_in_phase1
golden_path_tests.description: Verify adapter-backed actions return adapter_not_available before Phase 3
steps: In scope_ready, POST /actions/generate_master_plan (adapter-backed)
Verify response includes adapter_not_available status with plain English explanation
Verify state did not change (validate this is read-only endpoint behavior)
